{"step_by_step_thinking": "Tocilizumab is a humanized monoclonal antibody that targets the interleukin-6 (IL-6) receptor, not the tumor necrosis factor (TNF) alpha cytokine. The documents confirm that tocilizumab is an interleukin-6 inhibitor, as stated in Document [26], which lists tocilizumab and sarilumab as IL-6 inhibitors. In contrast, tumor necrosis factor (TNF) inhibitors include drugs like etanercept, infliximab, adalimumab, and golimumab, which target TNF-alpha. Document [26] explicitly differentiates tocilizumab from TNF inhibitors by classifying it as an IL-6 inhibitor. Therefore, tocilizumab is not a tumor necrosis factor inhibitor.", "answer_choice": "B"}
